items.q: What is the difference between lateral, medial and terminal moraine?
items.correct: Lateral moraine forms along the sides of a glacier from rockfall; medial moraine forms where two lateral moraines merge; terminal moraine marks the maximum extent at the snout.
items.misconception: Moraines are all the same thing — just piles of rocks.
items.random_wrong: Lateral moraine is the youngest and terminal moraine is the oldest.
items.why: Their position records glacial history. Students lump them as 'rocks'. Distinguishing them is essential — terminal moraines mark past climate maxima; medial moraines reveal tributary glaciers.

items.q: What is the difference between weather and climate?
items.correct: Weather is short-term atmospheric conditions at a specific time and place; climate is the long-term (typically 30-year) average of weather patterns in a region.
items.misconception: Weather happens outside and climate happens globally.
items.random_wrong: Weather refers to rain and climate refers to temperature.
items.why: Students treat them as different phenomena rather than different timescales. The key distinction is time — climate is statistical, weather is instantaneous.

items.correct: The combined loss of water from a surface through evaporation (from soil and water bodies) and transpiration (from plant leaves).
items.misconception: It is only water evaporating from puddles and lakes.
items.random_wrong: It is the process of rain falling from clouds.
items.why: Students focus only on evaporation. Transpiration from forests can move enormous quantities of water — a key concept for understanding deforestation's effect on the water cycle.
It's Variety of life. “Total population” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Correct answer: Protecting nature. “Cutting forests” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many UK national parks are there (approx)?
items.why: The real figure is 15. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
